text.title: Voice Agent Platforms: Selection and Comparison
text.summary: Platform comparison for managed voice agents (Retell, Vapi, ElevenLabs, LiveKit) with selection rules based on use case requirements.

rule.statement: Use Retell AI or Vapi for phone-based agents when time-to-production is the priority. Use ElevenLabs Conversational AI when voice quality is the primary differentiator. Use LiveKit only when self-hosted infrastructure is required.
rule.rationale: Managed platforms absorb the hardest problems: WebRTC setup, VAD tuning, telephony integration, billing per minute. LiveKit self-hosting requires real-time media server expertise and adds operational burden that is rarely worth it unless data residency requirements demand it.

list.item: Phone agents (inbound/outbound): Retell AI ($0.07-0.12/min) or Vapi ($0.05-0.10/min)
Premium voice quality: ElevenLabs Conversational AI (~500ms latency, $0.50/min)
Self-hosted required: LiveKit (OSS, WebRTC media server)
Rapid prototyping: Vapi (good webhook support, web playground)
Enterprise low latency: Synthflow (<100ms, custom pricing)

rule.statement: Use WebRTC for browser and mobile apps (lowest latency). Use SIP/PSTN for telephony integration. Budget for 100-200ms additional latency on PSTN calls vs WebRTC.
rule.rationale: PSTN adds carrier-grade latency on top of the AI pipeline. For real-time conversational feel, WebRTC is preferred where available. SIP trunking via Twilio or Retell bridges the gap for phone number requirements.

section.title: Pipeline Latency Targets
section.p: Total pipeline (STT + LLM + TTS) must stay under 600-800ms for natural conversation. Component targets:

antipattern.description: Using GPT-4o Realtime API (speech-to-speech) expecting tool-call support during streaming.
antipattern.reason: GPT-4o Realtime API has no tool-call support during streaming as of 2026. The model must finish speaking before tools execute, adding a full turn of latency. Use STT → LLM → TTS pipeline for tool-heavy voice agents.
